results.title: GISTI - Welcome
results.content: Welcome to the GISTI (Groupe d'information et de soutien des immigrés), the Group providing information and support to Immigrants, an independent non-profit

results.title: The Gisti?
results.content: organisation provides free legal assistance, publishes brochures and books and organises training programmes. You can receive expert legal

results.title: Gaia Alternatives
results.content:
- Depends on the use case. If you are a big hiker, AllTrails is worth a look. If on the road, not back country, Osmand , Rever, and MyRoute offer good navigation and planning functionality but at a price. Osmands web interface is not as good as Gaias, but is the least expensive For tourist trip travel, Roadtrippers, Wanderlog, and Sygic offer basic planning and navigation but are ~$30+ish subscriptions Gaia still has the best cross platform solution but it can be buggy and very slow to sync across devices. Search for basics like hotels and restaurants is especially poor. But as a poor mans GIS it is serviceable, and its good for both planning and navigation When my subscription is up, I am moving to Garmin's free basecamp for planning using GPX , and Maps Plus ios app as my on the road [...] - If they would just get their shit together with the offline maps, I'd be reasonably happy. Even better would be the ability to download an entire state at once instead of outlining sections, which can take a long time. My sub is up in July. If the don't fix the offline maps issue by then I'm a goner.
- Check out Natural Atlas. [...] ## Top Comments
- Outmap is a strong alternative: fits all the requirements: offline mode, desktop<>mobile sync, tablet app, records tracks, has a free version I started using it as an alternative to Fatmap and I preferred Fatmap to Gaia even earlier. 3D mode with quality sat imagery for me is a major differentiator. Also less bugs, Gaia is hard to live with
- Organic Maps: free, open source, osm-based, rather pleasantly looking map and interface. Not too many features, but the dev team is active and continuously improving it.
- give Backtrack a try:  (disclaimer: I made it)
